schema_notice: A3 adversarial literature matrix, machine-readable. See docs/RELATED_WORK_MATRIX.md for narrative analysis and docs/A3_SEARCH_LOG.md for search methodology. null/UNKNOWN used wherever primary-source evidence was insufficient; see 'uncertainties' per entry.

papers.title: CASK: Core-Aware Selective KV Compression for Reasoning Traces
evidence: Official repo file scripts/replay_reference_fidelity.py, fetched 2026-07-19: reference tokens are taken from a prior FullKV/high-budget run's own generated output (`reference_output = str(record.get('output', ''))`), tokenized, and at each decode step the loop feeds `target_token = continuation_ids[step_idx : step_idx + 1]` into the compressed-cache model via `input_ids=target_token.unsqueeze(0)` regardless of what the compressed model's own distribution would have produced -- strict teacher forcing over the fixed reference trace.

Same file: per-step statistics recorded include target-token log-probability, top-1/top-5 agreement, first-mismatch position, and realized cache size read from `past_key_values`/the compressor's own state -- i.e. continuation-fidelity and cache statistics recorded at every step, matching the N1 primitive description verbatim.

README (fetched 2026-07-19): 'teacher-forced replay harness' is listed as the repo's 'Main replay harness' and described as evaluation infrastructure, not the paper's contribution (the contribution is the CASK compression method: core/scratch partitioning).

Abstract (fetched 2026-07-19): 'CASK shows higher full-KV continuation fidelity than TriAttention at matched budgets on both AIME24 and AIME25' -- confirms reasoning-model, decode-time, FullKV-referenced comparison.

The replay loop consumes the ENTIRE reference continuation (`for step_idx in range(int(continuation_ids.numel()))`, no early break other than reaching the end) -- no early-answering or omitted-suffix intervention is implemented anywhere in this evaluation path.
papers.distinction: CASK's replay is a full-continuation fidelity/agreement measurement (does the compressed condition track the reference token-by-token, and by how much NLL), never an early-answering or omitted-suffix causal-dependence measurement -- it never truncates the visible trace and asks what the model does when forced to answer without the rest. It also reports no predeclared accuracy-neutral gate (fidelity numbers ARE the headline result, not a stop/continue screen checked before a separate causal experiment), and 'core vs scratch' is a method-design token classification used to build CASK's own compression policy, not a per-example post-hoc failure-mechanism taxonomy over held-out examples. Task domain is AIME24/AIME25 math plus summarization long-context sets (multi_news/vcsum/qmsum/gov_report), not GSM8K, and no accuracy-preserving/-neutral gate against a frozen ceiling is reported.
papers.verdict: Independently implements, and makes publicly available as of 2026-04-13 (three months before this repository's A2 commit), the exact fixed-generated-trace / teacher-forced / cache-state-varies diagnostic primitive this repository's N1 claims novelty for. Kills N1 outright. Substantially weakens broad N2 (it is explicitly a reasoning-model, decode-time-KV-eviction application of that primitive). Does not implement early-answering/omitted-suffix intervention, a predeclared accuracy-neutral gate, or held-out per-example mechanism classification, so it does not satisfy the full N3 package.
uncertainties: Only the abstract page and the specific replay script + README were fetched; the full PDF's experiments/appendix tables were not read in full, so any additional evaluation modes described only in-body (e.g. a possible held-out split) could not be confirmed or ruled out -- held_out_evaluation is marked UNKNOWN rather than NO for this reason.
Whether AIME24/AIME25 grading in the CASK harness itself constitutes an accuracy check at all (vs. pure token-fidelity) was not confirmed from the fetched excerpts.

papers.title: When Does Value-Aware KV Eviction Help? A Fixed-Contract Diagnostic for Non-Monotone Cache Compression
evidence: Abstract (fetched 2026-07-19): 'a fixed-contract diagnostic that holds the selector's setup fixed and changes one decision slot at a time' -- confirms a fixed-contract, one-variable-at-a-time diagnostic philosophy, but the object held fixed is the SELECTOR's setup/configuration, not a generated model output trace.
Abstract: 'On LongBench across three models and two budgets' -- LongBench is a long-context retrieval/QA/summarization benchmark, not a reasoning-CoT benchmark; no GSM8K/MATH/AIME mention found.

Abstract: 'A selector can fail at three steps: it may miss the evidence future decoding needs, give high scores to tokens that do not affect the output, or break related evidence when fitting scores into a small cache' -- frames the object of study as prefill/context evidence selection for later decode-time answer retrieval, not decode-time reasoning-trace compression.

'Not found after inspecting the abstract page and the targeted question set fetched 2026-07-19: no explicit statement that a generated reasoning trace is held fixed and teacher-forced across conditions was found; the fixed object described is the selector's own decision contract.'
papers.distinction: Shares only the abstract experimental-design philosophy ('hold most things fixed, vary one decision slot, observe the effect') with this repository's fixed-trace controls -- it does not hold a MODEL-GENERATED reasoning trace fixed across conditions, does not appear to study reasoning models or decode-time CoT-KV eviction (LongBench is retrieval/long-context QA/summarization), and does not implement an early-answering or omitted-CoT-suffix intervention. Its 'contract' is a selector-configuration control, not a token-trajectory control -- exactly the 'selector contract' vs. 'fixed generated trace' distinction the task instructions flagged as the key non-equivalence to check for.
papers.verdict: PARTIAL OVERLAP ONLY, as hypothesized. The fixed-contract *methodology* (hold most of an experimental configuration fixed, vary one slot, observe) is a reasonable generic diagnostic-design pattern this paper applies to context-selector correctness in long-context QA/retrieval; it does not implement FaithKV's fixed GENERATED TRACE control, does not study reasoning models, and does not implement early-answering/suffix-omission. It does not kill N1 (different object held fixed), does not kill N2 (not reasoning/not decode-time CoT KV eviction), and does not kill N3.
uncertainties: Full paper body/appendix not fetched -- only the abstract and a targeted-question WebFetch pass were available, so this classification could change if the full text describes a generated-trace-level fixed-contract variant not visible in the abstract.

decode_time_compression, matched_memory, and held_out_evaluation are UNKNOWN because the fetched excerpt did not specify whether the KV budget applies to prefill (context) tokens, decode-generated tokens, or both, nor whether LongBench splits used here are held-out relative to any calibration set.

papers.title: Hold Onto That Thought: Assessing KV Cache Compression On Reasoning
evidence: WebSearch summary (2026-07-19): benchmarks existing compression strategies (H2O, decoding-enabled SnapKV variant, etc.) on GSM8K/MATH500-style long-reasoning traces for a non-reasoning model (Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct) and reasoning models, reporting task accuracy per strategy -- an accuracy-comparison benchmark across methods, not a fixed-trace/teacher-forced replay diagnostic.
Finding cited: 'Eviction strategies at low budgets can produce longer reasoning traces' -- a natural-generation observation (each condition generates its own trace), not a same-trace comparison.
papers.distinction: This is a benchmark paper comparing final-accuracy outcomes of several existing KV-eviction methods on reasoning tasks under natural (unconstrained) generation -- structurally the same category of comparison as this repository's own retired protocol-v3 accuracy gate, not a fixed-trace or causal-intervention diagnostic. No teacher-forced replay, no early-answering, no per-example mechanism taxonomy beyond aggregate/dataset-level accuracy tables were found in the screened summary.
papers.verdict: Does not implement the N1 primitive, does not add a reasoning-specific application of it (N2), and is not part of the N3 combination beyond being general background evidence that KV-eviction-under-reasoning accuracy degradation is an active, recognized problem in the field.
uncertainties: Full paper/appendix not fetched; classification rests on a WebSearch summary only, per the search log's screened-tier confidence.

papers.title: R-KV: Redundancy-aware KV Cache Compression for Reasoning Models
papers.version_reviewed: as pinned in this repository's third_party/R-KV submodule, commit 45eaa7d69d20b7388321f077020a610d9afb65bd
evidence: This is the compression METHOD under test by this repository (pinned submodule), already audited with file:line precision in docs/UPSTREAM_AUDIT.md -- not re-audited here.

WebSearch summary (2026-07-19): reports aggregate accuracy retention at reduced KV budgets across math reasoning datasets under natural (unconstrained) generation -- no fixed-trace replay or causal suffix-omission intervention in the method paper itself.
papers.distinction: R-KV is the object of study in this repository, not a competing diagnostic -- its own paper reports standard aggregate accuracy-vs-budget curves, not any form of the fixed-trace/early-answering diagnostic this repository implements on top of R-KV.
papers.verdict: BACKGROUND. Cannot kill N1/N2/N3 since it contains no diagnostic of the relevant kind; included in the matrix only because Part 7 mandates it.

papers.title: ThinKV: Thought-Adaptive KV Cache Compression for Efficient Reasoning Models
authors: unconfirmed -- not extracted from screened summary
evidence: WebSearch summary (2026-07-19): 'attention sparsity reveals distinct thought types with varying importance within the CoT'; applies hybrid quantization-eviction assigning precision by thought importance; 'near-lossless accuracy with less than 5% of the original KV cache' on DeepSeek-R1-Distill, GPT-OSS, AceReason.
papers.distinction: A compression METHOD paper (thought-type-aware precision/eviction policy) reporting aggregate accuracy at reduced budget, structurally parallel to R-KV -- no fixed-trace replay, no causal intervention, no per-example failure taxonomy found in the screened summary.
papers.verdict: BACKGROUND. Does not overlap N1/N2/N3.
uncertainties: Full text not fetched; author list not confirmed.

papers.title: Value-Aware Stochastic KV Cache Eviction for Reasoning Models
evidence: WebSearch summary (2026-07-19): 'protects large-magnitude value states and promotes diverse eviction decisions'; 'evicting them causes catastrophic failure where models enter repetitive reasoning loops'; evaluated on six reasoning tasks with Qwen3 models at 4x compression, aggregate accuracy comparison against selection-based sparse attention baselines.
papers.distinction: A compression METHOD paper with a failure-mode OBSERVATION (repetitive loops from evicting high-magnitude value states) used to motivate the method design, not a fixed-trace or causal-suffix-omission diagnostic and not a per-example held-out mechanism taxonomy.
papers.verdict: BACKGROUND. Does not overlap N1/N2/N3.

papers.title: Measuring Faithfulness in Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
evidence: Well-established prior art (prior knowledge, corroborated by WebSearch 2026-07-19 abstract listing): introduces early answering (truncate the CoT at various points and force an answer), adding mistakes, paraphrasing, and filler-token interventions as CoT-faithfulness tests -- early answering specifically is the direct ancestor of this repository's 'branch at several fractions of the thinking span, close the thinking block, force a short final answer' protocol (CLAUDE.md Section 1).

These interventions operate at the TEXT/PROMPT level -- truncating and re-feeding the model's own decoded reasoning text -- with NO cache-policy variable and no KV compression of any kind; the model's context/KV state at inference time is simply whatever a standard forward pass over the truncated text produces, always under full, uncompressed attention.
papers.distinction: Lanham et al. is the original source of the early-answering intervention itself and of using match/no-match against an untruncated answer as a faithfulness/dependence signal -- this repository's CLAIM_BOUNDARY_NOTICE and its own restraint from calling the result a 'faithfulness' claim exists precisely because this prior work already showed why that stronger claim is unsupported. It contains no KV cache manipulation, no compressed-vs-FullKV comparison, and no accuracy-neutral operating-point gate -- it is a single-condition (always full attention) study.
papers.verdict: Kills the novelty of 'early answering as a chain-of-thought dependence/faithfulness intervention' as a general technique (this repository never claimed that part was novel per se -- CLAUDE.md's claim boundary already restricts the allowed conclusion to counterfactual behavioral dependence, language chosen partly because of exactly this prior work). Does not touch N1 (no fixed-trace KV-policy replay) or N3 (no KV compression at all) -- overlap is confined to the early-answering half of N2's application description.

papers.title: Measuring the Faithfulness of Thinking Drafts in Large Reasoning Models
evidence: WebSearch summary (2026-07-19): 'Draft-to-Answer Faithfulness... measures the extent to which a model's final answer is strictly derived from its thinking draft, comprising draft reliance (whether the answer-stage introduces substantial additional reasoning) and draft-answer consistency'.

'Intra-Draft Faithfulness... introducing counterfactual steps within the thinking draft and observing whether the model appropriately integrates or corrects them' -- a text-level counterfactual-editing intervention on the draft, not a cache-policy intervention.
papers.distinction: This paper's 'draft reliance' concept (does the answer-stage introduce substantial additional reasoning beyond the draft) is conceptually adjacent to this repository's early-answering/omitted-suffix dependence measurement, and both use a fixed draft/trace as the object being intervened on -- but the intervention here is textual counterfactual-step insertion/editing of the draft content itself, not substitution of the KV-cache compression policy while holding tokens fixed. No KV compression, no accuracy-preserving operating-point gate, no realized-memory measurement.
papers.verdict: Reinforces that 'is the final answer causally dependent on the visible draft' is an actively studied question via non-KV interventions (kills novelty of that general framing, which this repository does not claim anyway per its CLAIM_BOUNDARY_NOTICE). Does not touch KV compression at all, so no N1/N3 overlap; PARTIAL N2 overlap only on the 'reasoning model draft-to-answer dependence' half of the application description.

papers.title: RFEval: Benchmarking Reasoning Faithfulness under Counterfactual Reasoning Intervention in Large Reasoning Models
authors: unconfirmed -- not extracted from screened summary
papers.version_reviewed: v1/v3 mirrors both observed; exact version drawn from not confirmed
evidence: WebSearch summary (2026-07-19): 'defines reasoning faithfulness by two testable conditions: stance consistency... and causal influence (the stated reasoning causally drives the answer under output-level interventions), explicitly decoupled from accuracy'; benchmark of 7,186 instances across seven tasks (code, math, logic, table, context, legal, paper review); evaluates twelve open-source LRMs; finds unfaithfulness in 49.7% of outputs.
papers.distinction: A large benchmark explicitly measuring causal influence of reasoning on the answer via 'output-level interventions' across seven task domains including mathematical reasoning -- conceptually the closest screened paper to this repository's causal-dependence framing outside of Lanham/Thinking-Drafts. No evidence found of any KV-cache manipulation or compression-condition comparison; interventions are described as 'output-level', consistent with text/decoding-level perturbation rather than cache-state substitution.
papers.verdict: Reinforces (does not newly kill, since Lanham already established the category) that causal-influence-of-reasoning-on-answer is an actively benchmarked question at scale, decoupled from accuracy -- exactly the accuracy/faithfulness decoupling this repository's own claim-boundary discipline also insists on. No KV-compression axis, so no N1/N3 overlap.
uncertainties: Full text not fetched; exact intervention mechanics ('output-level interventions') not confirmed beyond the search summary phrase.

papers.title: Which Heads Matter for Reasoning? RL-Guided KV Cache Compression
evidence: WebSearch summary (2026-07-19): uses RL as a probe to find reasoning-critical attention heads, allocates full cache to those, aggressively compresses others; '20-50% cache reduction with near lossless performance'.
papers.distinction: A compression method paper (head-level budget allocation), aggregate-accuracy evaluation only.
papers.verdict: BACKGROUND. Does not overlap N1/N2/N3.

papers.title: KaVa: Latent Reasoning via Compressed KV-Cache Distillation
evidence: WebSearch summary (2026-07-19): trains a latent-reasoning student distilled from a teacher's compressed KV-cache trajectories via self-distillation -- a TRAINING method, not an inference-time diagnostic.
papers.distinction: A training/distillation method that consumes compressed-KV-cache information as a supervisory signal; not an evaluation/diagnostic protocol at all.
papers.verdict: BACKGROUND. Does not overlap N1/N2/N3.

papers.title: Tactic: Adaptive Sparse Attention with Clustering and Distribution Fitting for Long-Context LLMs
evidence: WebSearch summary (2026-07-19): general long-context sparse-attention method (dynamic token selection via clustering/distribution fitting); not specifically framed around reasoning models or CoT.
papers.distinction: General long-context inference efficiency method, not reasoning/CoT-specific, not a diagnostic.
papers.verdict: BACKGROUND. Does not overlap N1/N2/N3.

papers.title: LazyEviction: Lagged KV Eviction with Attention Pattern Observation for Efficient Long Reasoning
authors: Halo-949 GitHub org authors -- full list not extracted from screened summary
evidence: WebSearch summary (2026-07-19): identifies a 'Token Importance Recurrence' phenomenon (tokens regain high attention after several decoding steps) and proposes lagged, window-based eviction to avoid prematurely discarding them; 50-70% cache reduction, aggregate accuracy comparison.
papers.distinction: A compression method paper motivated by an attention-pattern observation; aggregate-accuracy evaluation, no fixed-trace or causal intervention diagnostic.
papers.verdict: BACKGROUND. Does not overlap N1/N2/N3.

papers.title: Can LLMs Maintain Fundamental Abilities under KV Cache Compression? (introduces ShotKV, KVFundaBench)
papers.version_reviewed: v1 observed; v4 mirror also indexed under a retitled HTML page ('Semantic Integrity Matters...')
evidence: WebSearch summary (2026-07-19): comprehensive empirical study of KV-compression effects across world knowledge, commonsense, ARITHMETIC REASONING, code, safety, long-context; introduces KVFundaBench; 'arithmetic reasoning tasks prove particularly sensitive to aggressive compression, with... performance drops of 17.4%-43.3%'; proposes ShotKV as a phase-aware (prefill vs. decode) compression method.
papers.distinction: A broad task-level accuracy-degradation study across many capability categories (arithmetic reasoning is one of several), with no fixed-trace replay, no causal early-answering-style intervention, and no reasoning-model-specific (long-CoT) focus -- the 'reasoning' tested is short arithmetic-fact style, not extended chain-of-thought.
papers.verdict: ADJACENT background evidence that KV compression degrades arithmetic-reasoning accuracy specifically (relevant motivating context for why an accuracy gate matters at all), but no diagnostic-primitive overlap with N1/N2/N3.
uncertainties: A retitled/updated version ('Semantic Integrity Matters: Benchmarking and Preserving High-Density Reasoning in KV Cache Compression', arxiv.org/html/2502.01941v4) was also observed under the same arXiv ID -- the relationship between the v1 title used by the task prompt and this v4 retitling was not resolved from search snippets alone.

papers.title: KIVI: A Tuning-Free Asymmetric 2bit Quantization for KV Cache
evidence: Screened only (not independently WebFetch-ed this session; see A3_SEARCH_LOG.md). WebSearch summary: 2-bit asymmetric KV quantization, 2.6x memory reduction, 3.47x throughput improvement, general LLM inference (not reasoning-model-specific).
papers.distinction: A quantization method paper, explicitly named out-of-scope for this repository's own method space (CLAUDE.md Section 1: 'Do not implement... KIVI'). No fixed-trace, no causal intervention.
papers.verdict: BACKGROUND, foundational. Does not overlap N1/N2/N3.
uncertainties: Not independently re-fetched this session; screened-tier confidence only, per A3_SEARCH_LOG.md.

papers.title: H2O: Heavy-Hitter Oracle for Efficient Generative Inference of Large Language Models
evidence: Screened only (not independently WebFetch-ed this session). WebSearch summary: dynamic submodular formulation of eviction, retains recent + 'heavy hitter' tokens; foundational pre-reasoning-model-era eviction method (2023, OPT models).
papers.distinction: Foundational general-purpose eviction baseline, predates the reasoning-model KV-compression literature this repository engages with; also used as a baseline method inside 'Hold Onto That Thought' (2512.12008) above.
papers.verdict: BACKGROUND, foundational. Does not overlap N1/N2/N3.
uncertainties: Not independently re-fetched this session; screened-tier confidence only.

papers.title: Efficient Streaming Language Models with Attention Sinks
evidence: Screened only (not independently WebFetch-ed this session). WebSearch summary: sliding-window + attention-sink retention for infinite streaming generation; perplexity/streaming-stability focused, not reasoning-CoT focused, no faithfulness/dependence measurement.
papers.distinction: Foundational streaming-inference eviction baseline; unrelated to reasoning faithfulness or causal dependence measurement.
papers.verdict: BACKGROUND, foundational. Does not overlap N1/N2/N3.
uncertainties: Not independently re-fetched this session; screened-tier confidence only.

papers.title: Thought Branches: Interpreting LLM Reasoning Requires Resampling
evidence: WebSearch summary (2026-07-19): 'measuring a partial CoT's impact through resampling only the subsequent text'; introduces a 'resilience metric' via repeated resampling to test whether removing a reasoning step causes similar content to reappear; applies causal mediation analysis to unfaithful-CoT (hint) cases.
papers.distinction: Measures causal influence of a PARTIAL chain-of-thought on the eventual output via RESAMPLING many continuations from a fixed PREFIX (not full fixed continuation, not KV-cache-level intervention) -- mechanically closer to a Monte-Carlo ablation-and-resample design than to fixed-trace teacher-forced cache-policy replay. No KV compression axis at all.
papers.verdict: Reinforces (does not newly kill) that causal-dependence-of-answer-on-partial-reasoning is an active interpretability research direction using resampling rather than KV-cache substitution -- PARTIAL N2 overlap on the 'reasoning model, causal dependence on partial CoT' framing only; no N1/N3 overlap since no cache-policy variable exists in this design.

papers.title: Early Stopping Chain-of-thoughts in Large Language Models (ES-CoT)
evidence: WebSearch summary (2026-07-19): ES-CoT is an INFERENCE-TIME EFFICIENCY method that stops CoT generation once consecutive per-step 'step answers' converge, to save inference cost -- not a diagnostic or faithfulness study. A separate paper (Fast KVzip, arXiv 2601.17668, surfaced in round-3 search) compares against 'the early-stopping-of-thinking method' as a baseline that 'drastically reduces reasoning performance', suggesting ES-CoT-style methods are used elsewhere as accuracy-cost baselines, not as causal probes.
papers.distinction: 'Early stopping' here means truncating generation for EFFICIENCY once the model's own answer has converged (a deployment optimization), which is a different operation from this repository's 'early answering' (force an answer at a fraction of the trace regardless of convergence, to measure dependence on the omitted suffix) -- confusable by name only.
papers.verdict: No diagnostic overlap; included because of superficial name proximity ('early stopping' vs. 'early answering') that a reviewer might otherwise conflate.

papers.title: FaithCoT-Bench: Benchmarking Instance-Level Faithfulness of Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
authors: unconfirmed -- not extracted from screened summary
evidence: WebSearch summary (2026-07-19): FINE-CoT, an expert-annotated dataset of 1,000+ trajectories across four domains/four LLMs, benchmarking instance-level unfaithfulness DETECTION methods (counterfactual, logit-based, LLM-as-judge) against human ground truth.
papers.distinction: A DETECTION-method benchmark (given a trajectory, decide if it's unfaithful, compared against expert human labels) rather than an INTERVENTION-based causal diagnostic that this repository and Lanham/RFEval/Thinking-Drafts implement; no KV-cache axis.
papers.verdict: Background context establishing that instance-level CoT faithfulness classification is an active ICLR-2026-era research area; no diagnostic-primitive overlap with N1/N2/N3.
uncertainties: Full text not fetched; exact intervention/detection mechanics not confirmed beyond the search summary.
